div.head: 259. Memorandum of ConversationSource: Ford Library, National Security Adviser, Memoranda of Conversations, Box 5. Secret; Nodis. The meeting was held in the Oval Office.

list.head: PARTICIPANTS
list.item: Dr. Henry A. Kissinger, Secretary of State and Assistant to the President for National Security Affairs
Lt. General Brent Scowcroft, Deputy Assistant to the President for National Security Affairs

div.p: Kissinger: The Kurds are conducting a guerrilla war inside Iraq. If Iraq weren’t tied down, they could put more into Syria. [Described the program.]Brackets in the original.
The danger is the Kurds will collapse. We have exhausted our [less than 1 line not declassified] equipment. [2 lines not declassified]
President: What does this do to our argument about supplying out of active units?
Kissinger: The numbers aren’t that significant.

Kissinger: The Iranians have asked about sending troops in. The CIA guy is opposed. We said it is their decisionTelegram 24914 from Tehran, August 24, informed Kissinger of Barzani’s request for Iranian troops with anti-tank and anti-aircraft weapons to join his forces. (Ford Library, National Security Adviser, Backchannel Messages, Box 4, Mideast/Africa, Incoming 8/74) Kissinger replied in backchannel message WH42555, August 24: “This must be basically an Iranian decision. We will understand whatever they decide to do.” (Ibid., Outgoing 8/74)—I favor it but I hate to be on record because of all the leaks.
President: I read that the Iraqi army is unhappy. Are the Kurds reliable?
Kissinger: Yes. It is their existence at stake.
[Omitted here is discussion unrelated to Iraq.]
